data.title: SpaceX IPO Makes Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire While Dogecoin Price Tests a Potential Breakout Setup
data.description:
At the same time, Dogecoin is approaching a critical technical level that analysts say could determine its next major move.

SpaceX launched a record-breaking IPO valuing the company at nearly $1.8 trillion, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper. Source: Channel 4 News via X

The combination of one of the largest corporate milestones ever and growing interest in meme coins has fueled discussion among traders, particularly as Dogecoin's chart structure begins to align with areas that have historically preceded major price expansions.

SpaceX IPO Delivers Record-Breaking Market Debut

SpaceX officially entered public markets on June 12, 2026, in what became the largest initial public offering ever recorded.

The aerospace and satellite company sold approximately 555.6 million shares at $135 each, raising $75 billion and securing a valuation approaching $1.8 trillion before trading began.

More than 500 million SpaceX shares traded on the debut day, generating over $80 billion in volume and making the company the world's seventh-largest public firm by market value. Source: The Kobeissi Letter via X

Investor demand proved exceptionally strong, with shares opening above their IPO price and finishing the session roughly 20% higher.

According to reports, more than 500 million shares changed hands during the first trading day, generating over $80 billion in trading volume. The strong debut immediately positioned SpaceX among the largest publicly traded companies in the world.

Elon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's IPO valued the company at nearly $2 trillion, lifting the combined value of his SpaceX and Tesla stakes above $1 trillion. Source: Financial Times via X

The successful listing also had a significant impact on Elon Musk's net worth. Thanks to his substantial ownership stakes in both SpaceX and Tesla, Musk's combined holdings surpassed the trillion-dollar threshold, making him the first individual to achieve that milestone on paper.

The event has also intensified investor focus on future mega-listings, with companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic frequently mentioned as potential candidates for major public offerings in the coming years.

Dogecoin Price Tests Long-Term Support Zone

While markets digested the implications of the SpaceX IPO, analysts monitoring the crypto sector pointed to an important development involving Dogecoin.

According to market analyst Ali Martinez, Dogecoin is currently trading near a key structural support area that has historically acted as a foundation for larger bullish cycles.

Dogecoin is testing the $0.081 lower boundary of a five-year consolidation channel, a structure that has historically preceded major bull market expansions. Source: Ali Martinez via X

The analyst noted that DOGE is testing the $0.081 level, a region that coincides with the lower mid-range boundary of a five-year parallel trading channel that has guided price action since 2021.

Historically, Dogecoin has spent extended periods consolidating within broad ranges before transitioning into stronger uptrends. The current setup resembles previous phases in which volatility compressed before significant directional moves emerged.

Ali Charts stated:

"Dogecoin is currently trading at a critical structural inflection point."

The observation has attracted attention because similar tests of long-term channel support have previously occurred before major trend reversals.

On-Chain Data Shows Significant Demand Near $0.081

Beyond the chart structure, blockchain data suggests the current support area carries notable significance.

The UTXO Realized Price Distribution (URPD) metric, which tracks the price levels where circulating tokens last moved on-chain, reveals a large concentration of Dogecoin activity around $0.081.

URPD data shows more than 30 billion DOGE were last moved near $0.081, establishing the level as a major support zone backed by significant historical investor activity. Source: Ali Martinez via X

Data highlighted by Ali Charts shows that more than 30 billion DOGE tokens were last transacted near this level. Such concentrations often create psychological and technical support because many holders have established positions within that range.

The analyst explained that this cluster represents a major area of historical spot exposure, making it a key zone to watch as traders evaluate whether buyers can maintain control.

Another noteworthy development is the activity among large holders. Over the past week, whale wallets reportedly accumulated more than 200 million DOGE, suggesting continued interest from larger market participants despite recent price weakness.

Dogecoin Price Prediction Hinges on Key Support Levels

From a technical standpoint, the $0.081 area remains the most important level in the near term.

If buyers successfully defend this support zone, analysts believe Dogecoin could remain positioned for a broader recovery toward higher levels within its long-term channel structure. Such a move would reinforce the historical pattern of consolidation phases preceding stronger upward trends.

However, the bullish outlook is not guaranteed.

A sustained weekly close below $0.081 could weaken the current setup and expose the next major support level near $0.058, which represents the lower boundary of the multi-year channel.

Rather than focusing on short-term volatility, Ali Charts suggested a disciplined accumulation strategy around these high-conviction zones, arguing that the area between $0.081 and $0.058 has historically offered attractive long-term entry opportunities.

Dogecoin Outlook Remains Closely Watched

The latest Dogecoin price prediction narrative arrives at a time when investor attention is increasingly focused on Elon Musk following SpaceX's landmark IPO.

While there is no direct connection between the company's public listing and Dogecoin's technical structure, Musk's influence on market sentiment has historically drawn additional interest to the meme cryptocurrency.

Dogecoin (DOGE) was trading at around $0.87, up 0.81% in the last 24 hours at press time. Source: Brave New Coin

For now, Dogecoin remains at a pivotal point. The combination of long-term channel support, substantial on-chain activity near $0.081, and recent whale accumulation suggests that traders are closely monitoring whether the current floor can hold.

If support remains intact, the setup could provide the foundation for a larger recovery phase. If it fails, market participants may shift their attention toward lower support zones before any sustainable uptrend can emerge.

data.title: KIMS board approves preferential issue of 77 lakh warrants worth ₹600 cr to promoters
data.description:
Hospital chain Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Ltd (KIMS) on Saturday (June 13) approved a preferential issue of 77.02 lakh warrants to members of its promoter and promoter group, raising up to ₹600 crore.

The board approved the issuance of warrants at ₹779 apiece, including a premium of ₹777 per warrant. Each warrant is convertible into one fully paid-up equity share of face value ₹2 and can be exercised in one or more tranches within 18 months from the date of allotment.

The proposed allottees are promoter Dr Abhinay Bollineni, who will receive 32.09 lakh warrants, promoter Adwik Bollineni, who will receive 32.09 lakh warrants, and promoter group entity Bharas Ventures LLP, which will receive 12.84 lakh warrants.

The aggregate consideration receivable from the issue is about ₹600 crore. Dr Abhinay Bollineni and Adwik Bollineni will each contribute about ₹250 crore, while Bharas Ventures LLP will contribute ₹100 crore.

The company said the warrant issue price of ₹779 per warrant is not less than the floor price determined under applicable SEBI regulations and is based on a valuation report from a registered valuer. The relevant date for determining the issue price was June 9, 2026.

Following full conversion of the warrants, Dr Abhinay Bollineni’s shareholding will increase to 34.46 lakh shares from 2.36 lakh shares, while Adwik Bollineni's shareholding will rise to 32.50 lakh shares from 40,640 shares.

Bharas Ventures LLP, which currently holds no shares, will hold 12.84 lakh shares after conversion. The promoter and promoter group’s overall shareholding is expected to increase to 35.35% from 34.11%, assuming full conversion of the warrants.

Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences said 25% of the warrant issue price will be payable at the time of subscription and allotment, with the remaining 75% payable upon conversion. If the warrants are not exercised within 18 months, the entitlement will lapse, and the amount paid will be forfeited.

On Friday (June 12), shares of Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Ltd ended at ₹787.10, up by ₹2.88, or 22.05%, on the BSE.

data.title: China issues guidelines on financial services data amid broader cybersecurity push
data.description:
China's cybersecurity administrators on Saturday moved to tighten the grading and classification of data in the financial information services sector in what it said would strengthen data security management and regulate industry development.

  • The Cyberspace Administration of China said data would be classified into four levels-core, important, sensitive general and routine general-based on importance, sensitivity and the potential harm from leaks.

  • The move was announced jointly with six other departments, including the People’s Bank of China.

  • China has in recent years strengthened its data security framework, moving from top-level legislation to more detailed, sector-specific rules.

  • "Financial information services are developing in an orderly manner, and the volume of data is expanding ... which urgently requires standardised, classified and graded management," the guidelines said.

  • The rules do not apply to data involving state secrets or military information.

data.title: Standard Chartered Says Bitcoin Bottomed Near $59,000 As Crypto Winter Ends
data.description:
TL;DR

  • Standard Chartered’s Geoffrey Kendrick reportedly says Bitcoin’s $59,000 area marked the cycle bottom.
  • The note cites SpaceX IPO-related capital rotation and easing oil-price pressure as key catalysts.
  • Kendrick reportedly maintains a $100,000 year-end Bitcoin target and a $4,000 Ethereum target.

Standard Chartered Calls Bitcoin’s $59,000 Low The Cycle Bottom

Standard Chartered analyst Geoffrey Kendrick has reportedly called Bitcoin’s recent $59,000 area the cycle bottom, arguing that the latest downturn has ended and that crypto has moved into a new recovery phase.

The research note dated June 12 frames the low near $59,375 as a 53% retracement from Bitcoin’s all-time high of $126,000, set on Oct. 6, 2025. Kendrick’s quoted line — “Winter is over. Welcome back to crypto Spring.” — captures the tone of the call.

The bank’s year-end targets remain bullish, with Bitcoin at $100,000 and Ethereum at $4,000. Kendrick also expects ETH to outperform BTC as the recovery develops.

Why SpaceX And Oil Matter To The Bitcoin Call

The argument is not based on price structure alone. Kendrick reportedly links the bottom call to two catalysts: the completion of the SpaceX IPO and progress around a US-Iran peace deal that could cap oil prices.

The SpaceX angle is unusual but important. The research note argues that more than $5.72 billion of spot Bitcoin ETF redemptions since mid-May reflected investors liquidating crypto exposure to free capital for the SpaceX IPO. With the IPO completed, that specific drain may fade.

The oil angle is macro-driven. Lower Brent and WTI crude prices, reportedly around $87 and $85 a barrel, could reduce inflation pressure, cool Treasury yields and improve the liquidity backdrop for risk assets, including crypto.

The Confirmation Checklist

Kendrick is watching three confirmation signals: a return to net positive US spot Bitcoin ETF inflows, renewed corporate treasury buying and falling oil prices. That gives the call a useful framework rather than a simple bullish headline.

The risk is that all three inputs can change quickly. ETF flows can remain negative if sentiment stays weak, corporate treasury buying can disappoint, and geopolitics around Iran remains volatile. A later shift in policy headlines or oil prices could weaken the bottom thesis.

The key point is that Standard Chartered is treating $59,000 as a macro and flow-based bottom, not just a chart level. That makes the next round of ETF data and oil-price movement especially important for traders watching the call.

This report is based on primary-source material and supporting market data. View the Standard Chartered research portal.

The call also matters because it gives traders a clean level to test. If Bitcoin holds above the $59,000 area and ETF flows stabilize, the bottom thesis gains credibility. If the market loses that zone again, the argument that the cycle low is already locked in becomes harder to defend.

The next signal is likely to come from flows rather than slogans. A recovery in spot ETF demand, combined with calmer oil markets and renewed corporate treasury buying, would support Kendrick’s view that the selling pressure was temporary rather than structural.

Based on Standard Chartered market commentary attributed to Geoffrey Kendrick at Standard Chartered

data.title: Coinbase Set to Make a Major Announcement on June 16 – Details
data.description:
Coinbase is preparing to share what comes next in its effort to reshape itself from a crypto exchange into a broader financial platform. The company has scheduled a presentation for June 16, where it will unveil the next phase of its “Everything Exchange” strategy, a plan that aims to bring multiple financial services under one roof.

The announcement caught investors’ attention and briefly pushed Coinbase stock higher. COIN shares rose more than 2.6% during trading as markets reacted to the company’s long-term growth plans. However, enthusiasm later cooled as investors shifted their focus to the buzz surrounding the upcoming SpaceX IPO.

Coinbase 🛡️
@coinbase

Financial services are stuck in the past.

Markets that close.

Settlement that takes days.

Assets that are trapped in separate accounts.

We're building the alternative: one account for everything. Instant. 24/7.@Maxbranzburg, Head of Consumer & Business Products, lays it out. https://t.co/Esyaw00hMm

Jun 12, 2026

Building a Financial Hub Beyond Crypto

Coinbase’s latest vision is much bigger than buying and selling cryptocurrencies.

The company wants to create a platform where users can manage nearly all their financial activities through a single account. That includes crypto trading, stocks, ETFs, commodities, derivatives, payments, and lending services.

“We are building the financial services platform of the future at Coinbase: one account for everything you trade, settled instantly, open 24/7.” Coinbase Said.

In its latest update on X, Max Branzburg argued that much of the traditional financial system still operates on outdated infrastructure. Delayed settlements, limited trading hours, and slow money transfers remain common problems for users.

By using blockchain technology, Coinbase says financial markets can become faster and more efficient. Instead of waiting days for transactions to clear, assets could settle almost instantly while markets remain open around the clock.

What’s the plan?

Coinbase’s expansion plans are being driven by three key products. Perpetual futures will support 24/7 leveraged trading, while its Coinbase One subscription offers up to 3.5% APY on USD Coin holdings used as collateral or waiting for orders to execute. Moreover, the company is also expanding access to decentralized exchanges directly through the Coinbase app. Alongside this, Coinbase Advisor uses AI and company data to help users analyze portfolios and market opportunities, with plans to allow customers to deploy their own AI agents to trade automatically.

With the June 16 presentation approaching, investors will be watching closely to see how Coinbase plans to connect crypto, traditional finance, and AI into a single platform. If successful, the company could position itself as a one-stop destination for trading and financial services rather than just a cryptocurrency exchange.
